purpose: Add one or more KC forms to a binder. Batch is native (body is array). Forms land in Unfiled KC Forms.
form_object_fields_constant_or_placehold: Documented in scripts/kc.py:DEFAULT_FORM_BODY. Server populates real GUIDs in response.
response_shape: {"result": [...], "statusCode": ..., "message": ...}. `result` is an array that ECHOES every SUCCESSFULLY-added form, each with its server-assigned `workpaperId`/`id`/`wState` - it is NOT failures-only and it is NOT empty on full success. (The earlier doc claim that `result` lists only failed/flagged forms and that successful adds are silent is FALSE - do not reintroduce it.)
success_check: Match each planned form's `referenceTag` to a `result[]` entry; that entry's `workpaperId` is the new form's id. Success is DIRECTLY VISIBLE in `result[]` - you do not need a separate GetBinder to confirm an add succeeded (though a re-GET still satisfies the universal re-GET-after-write doctrine and catches referenceTag mismatches).
gotchas: The `index` field in the body is SILENTLY IGNORED. Set indexes via wpm_set_index endpoint after move.
A form whose `referenceTag` is unrecognized simply will NOT appear in `result[]` - diff your planned referenceTags against the echoed ones to spot any that were skipped.

NEVER blind-retry the POST. Rationale is NOT 'successes are invisible' (they are visible in `result[]`) - it is that re-POSTing creates DUPLICATE forms (e.g. 3 POSTs = duplicate COR-216/KBA-303). If a parse looked confusing, read `result[]` for the echoed adds before retrying; on any genuine retry, pre-POST dedup-diff against the already-added referenceTags.
